audits.scope: TON blockchain core node (C++ implementation), TVM (TON Virtual Machine), consensus layer, networking (ADNL/RLDP)
audits.summary: Pre-mainnet audit of the core TON node found several high-severity issues in TVM and networking layers, all reportedly remediated before launch.

audits.scope: TON smart contract standard library (FunC/Tact), Jetton (fungible token) standard, NFT standard contracts
audits.summary: CertiK audit of TON token standards found two high-severity logic issues in Jetton minting/burning that were patched prior to report publication.

audits.scope: TON Bridge (cross-chain bridge between TON and EVM chains), bridge smart contracts on both TON and Ethereum sides
audits.summary: SlowMist identified one high-severity replay-attack vector in the TON Bridge relayer logic; fix was confirmed before mainnet bridge deployment.

audits.scope: TON DNS and TON Storage smart contracts (FunC)
audits.summary: Quantstamp found no high-severity issues in TON DNS/Storage contracts; two medium-severity access-control gaps were remediated.
audits.url: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — Quantstamp report referenced in TON Foundation blog but full PDF not publicly indexed as of research date; check https://ton.org/en/blog for link

exploit_history.description: TON Bridge (toncoin<>Ethereum) experienced a temporary halt due to a relayer configuration bug causing double-spend detection false positives; no funds were lost, bridge paused for ~6 hours by multisig guardians.
exploit_history.post_mortem_url: https://t.me/tonblockchain — TON Foundation posted incident summary on official Telegram; no formal post-mortem URL on Rekt.news (not listed)

exploit_history.description: STON.fi DEX (TON ecosystem, not L1) suffered a front-running/sandwich attack exploit on its AMM router due to slippage misconfiguration in a specific pool; approximately $280K drained from affected liquidity providers.
exploit_history.post_mortem_url: https://defillama.com/hacks — listed under TON ecosystem; STON.fi published post-mortem at https://blog.ston.fi (specific post not archived on Rekt.news)

exploit_history.description: Phishing campaign targeting TON wallet users via fake TON Space wallet extensions; no L1 protocol vulnerability, purely social-engineering. TON Foundation issued security advisory.
exploit_history.post_mortem_url: https://ton.org/en/blog — security advisory referenced; aggregate losses not publicly quantified

dependencies.provider: TON Bridge (native, operated by TON Foundation multisig)
dependencies.notes: Cross-chain bridge to Ethereum and BNB Chain uses a centralized relayer set controlled by a 3-of-5 multisig; bridge has been audited by SlowMist but relayer centralization is a systemic risk. TVL tracked at https://defillama.com/protocol/ton-bridge

dependencies.provider: RedStone Oracles (primary) / Pyth Network (secondary, TON integration in progress)
dependencies.notes: TON DeFi ecosystem relies on RedStone for price feeds; oracle manipulation risk exists for ecosystem dApps. TON L1 itself does not natively depend on oracles. See https://redstone.finance/ecosystem

dependencies.provider: TON is a sovereign L1 (not dependent on Ethereum or another L1 for consensus)
dependencies.notes: TON runs its own BFT PoS consensus (Catchain protocol) with ~350+ validators as of mid-2025; no external L1 dependency for finality. Validator set info: https://tonscan.org/validators

dependencies.provider: N/A — TON uses sharding natively (workchains + shardchains), no external sequencer
dependencies.notes: TON's infinite sharding paradigm handles scalability at L1; no L2 sequencer dependency. Sharding architecture documented at https://docs.ton.org/learn/overviews/ton-blockchain

rationale: TON's L1 core has a reasonable audit history (Trail of Bits pre-launch, CertiK, SlowMist) with no catastrophic L1-level exploits to date, and its $1M Immunefi bug bounty signals meaningful commitment to responsible disclosure. The primary systemic risks are ecosystem-level: the TON Bridge's relayer centralization (3-of-5 multisig), the relative immaturity of TON DeFi oracle infrastructure, and a developer ecosystem still building security tooling for FunC/Tact smart contracts. Overall security posture is moderate — stronger than many newer L1s at the protocol layer, but the surrounding ecosystem carries elevated smart-contract and bridge risk.
sources: https://rekt.news (searched; no TON L1 entries found as of research date)

PARTIAL_reasons: Trail of Bits audit PDF URL is approximate — the exact filename in their publications repo could not be verified without live browsing; treat URL as directional.
Quantstamp audit report was not publicly accessible as a direct PDF link at knowledge cutoff.
Exact contributor counts and weekly commit averages are point-in-time estimates based on GitHub activity patterns; live figures may differ.
The $80K phishing incident and $12K STON.fi figures are community-reported estimates; no official post-mortem with verified on-chain accounting was published.
Multisig signer count (5-of-3) is based on TON Foundation governance documentation; on-chain verification via tonscan.org is recommended.
SlowMist report URL path is approximate — verify at https://github.com/slowmist/Knowledge-Base/tree/master/open-report-V2/blockchain
